name: Pane e Vino
signature: Honest Italian on Mount Martha's village strip  -  antipasti boards, wood-fired pizza, a short pasta list, and a loyal bayside local following
editorNote:
Pane e Vino is Mount Martha's quiet Italian  -  a small room on the village strip that has been feeding the bayside locals reliably for years and deserves to be better known outside the suburb. The menu is direct: antipasti boards built around good charcuterie and olives, a short wood-fired pizza list, a handful of pasta classics, and a seasonal main or two. The wine list is tight and leans into Italian bottles at honest prices.

What it does better than most of the Peninsula's bigger-name trattorias is the quiet-Sunday-night side of the Italian restaurant contract. Walk in without a booking, take a table by the window, order a pizza and a glass of Chianti, and let the evening settle. The bayside at Mount Martha rewards exactly this kind of unhurried local meal.

It fills up when the weather turns warm. Book on weekends.
